target: docs/assets/eclipse-stations-vs-penumbra-band.svg — referenced from §4.2.7a (sections.json .part4.html). EXP-260 c1 H0 [critical] flagged this asset as missing.
finding: OBE — asset already exists on main and is mathematically correct. No new SVG needed; ISS-1650 should close as resolved-no-action.

timeline.obe_cause: Decider reading curmudgeon review without re-checking whether workspace-sync had already pushed the asset between curmudgeon-review-time and decider-run-time. Same OBE pattern as Mode 2b — stale curmudgeon flag treated as live state.

math_verification.claim: SVG correctly projects 9 stations using AE-from-N at R_disc=20015 km
math_verification.verified: Sanity-checked Ebro and Canary positions. Ebro lat 40.957, lon 0.333 → r_disc = (90-40.957)*20015/90 = 10906.6 km. Expected SVG x = 97.2 + 10906.6*0.04796 = 620.3 px (SVG has 620.2). Expected y = 396.7 - 10906.6*0.04796*sin(0.333°) = 393.7 px (SVG has 393.7). Canary lat 28.32, lon -16.43 → r_disc = 13716.9 km. Expected x = 728.2 px (SVG has 728.1). Expected y = 396.7 + 13716.9*0.04796*|sin(-16.43°)| = 582.8 px (SVG has 582.8). Both stations reproduce to within 0.1 px. Convention is AE-from-N with rim-at-equator (matches EXP-260 expansion file convention).

math_verification.claim: Per-station path distances match the EXP-260 table
math_verification.verified: Reproduced via Python point-to-segment distance against the SVG polyline. Ebro min-distance 444 km (matches 444 ✓), Canary 4,084 km (matches 4,084 ✓). Closest is Ebro (10.6× R_p), furthest is Canary (97× R_p). Numbers match EXP-260 c1 curmudgeon math_reproduction exactly.

Visual elements present: 9 red station dots (all OUTSIDE band, per legend convention), orange umbra path polyline, scale-rings at 2.5/5/7.5/10/12.5/15k km, distance labels next to each station, header text "Dome geometry vs. dome’s own 9-station list", green callout box with "Result: 0 of 9 stations within 42-km penumbra radius". Section's tag in sections.json .part4.html (and rendered docs/index.html) points at this exact path.

holes_addressed: EXP-260 c1 H0 [critical] — missing SVG asset. Already resolved by an earlier workspace-sync run that committed bda7f5b on 2026-04-27T18:27:07Z; verified asset is present, math correct, and referenced correctly from the live section.
holes_NOT_addressed_in_this_exp: EXP-260 c1 H1 [moderate] — alternative AE convention (rim-at-equator vs rim-at-S-pole). Out of scope for EXP-273 (asset task only). Should be tracked separately if not already.
EXP-260 c1 H2 [moderate] — physical-altitude robustness. Out of scope.
EXP-260 c1 H3 [moderate] — NN-tour starting-point dependence. Out of scope.
EXP-260 c1 H4-H6 [minor] — linearization caveat, SNK code substitution, sample-point reproducibility footnote. Out of scope.
recommendation_to_decider: Close ISS-1650 with status=resolved, fix_date=2026-04-27T18:27:07Z (the bda7f5b commit), resolved_by=workspace-sync (auto-resolved before issue was opened). Mark EXP-273 status=integrated (no new files needed). The other six EXP-260 holes (H1-H6) remain open if not already tracked under separate issues; analyst recommends opening one or two follow-up expansions for H1+H2 (the two moderate holes that strengthen the section) on the next run if no other priority items are queued.
consider_other_side: Counter-argument: maybe the decider intentionally re-fired ISS-1650 because it found a defect in the SVG (corrupt, math-wrong, points at wrong path, etc.). Tested by independent reproduction of station projection and per-station path distance: every checked value matches the EXP-260 table exactly. Counter rejected — the SVG is correct as committed; the issue is genuinely OBE.
decider_instructions: Verify the SVG is present at docs/assets/eclipse-stations-vs-penumbra-band.svg and md5=5d76b683d488b4e7c63936506bbd3dc3 (or any later valid revision).

Move ISS-1650 from open-issues to closed-issues with: status=resolved-no-action, fix_date=2026-04-27T18:27:07Z, fixed_at=2026-04-27T18:27:07Z, fixed_by=workspace-sync (commit bda7f5b), resolved_reason="Asset was already on main when ISS-1650 was created; analyst (EXP-273) verified file presence, content correctness, and reference linkage."
Mark EXP-273 status=integrated in expansion-tracker.json (no integration needed beyond the existing commit).
Optional: schedule a follow-up EXP for the other EXP-260 c1 holes (H1 alternative AE convention, H2 physical-altitude robustness) — both would strengthen §4.2.7a per the curmudgeon review.
acceptance_criteria: No new files committed (asset was already in main).
docs/index.html still renders §4.2.7a with the SVG image (verified: img tag present in built output).
out_of_scope_explicit: Other EXP-260 c1 holes (H1-H6). Re-rendering or improving the existing SVG. Verifying the section's prose against the alternative AE convention.
notes_for_decider: This is an OBE-pattern issue — workspace-sync committed the asset 11h before the decider opened ISS-1650. The decider should either (a) accept this as a normal race condition between curmudgeon flag-time and asset-availability, or (b) consider adding a pre-issue-create check that re-tests the curmudgeon claim against current main state for asset_creation issues. Pattern-tag for tinker if a habitual problem.
